_meta.note: Daemon-backed multiplexed, lossless, blocking pull (Phase 3). Drains matched events across all in-scope buckets for one subscription, server-advancing the per-bucket offsets so the next pull continues where this one stopped. Blocks up to timeout_ms (omitted = DEFAULT_PULL_TIMEOUT_MS 5000; clamped to [1, MAX_PULL_TIMEOUT_MS 8000]) until events arrive or the timeout elapses. max is clamped to MAX_PULL_EVENTS (omitted = 50). Returns source-tagged events[] (each carrying bucket_id, optional job_id, the per-bucket seq, and the full SignalEvent) plus per-source liveness[] (starting|running|exited|failed|cancelled|stopped|dropped), and the lagged and truncated flags. US4/FR-031: the adapter always requests the liveness DELTA, so liveness[] carries the full snapshot on a subscription's FIRST pull and right after any subscription_seek, then only entries whose state changed since the last pull; the section is OMITTED entirely when the delta is empty (a steady idle pull). A transition is present in exactly the next pull. An IDLE pull is SUCCESS with empty events[] (and liveness present only as a non-empty delta), NEVER an error. lagged=true means an in-scope bucket evicted events under the consumer; truncated=true means the routing scan hit MAX_BUCKETS_PER_SUBSCRIPTION. An unknown/expired/malformed sub_id returns the unknown_subscription IPC code (re-open). The MCP client waits longer than the daemon timeout so the idle path is empty-success, never a JSON-RPC timeout.

response_example_idle._note: An idle pull (no new events before the daemon timeout elapsed) is SUCCESS, not an error: events[] is empty. US4/FR-031: liveness is a DELTA, so a steady idle pull whose sources did not change OMITS the liveness section entirely (shown here). The FIRST pull after open/seek instead carries the full liveness baseline. The agent simply loops subscription_pull again.

response_example_idle_first_pull_baselin._note: The FIRST pull after open (or after any subscription_seek) carries the full per-source liveness snapshot as the delta baseline, even when no events are ready.

response_example_lagged_truncated._note: lagged=true means at least one in-scope bucket evicted events under the consumer (FIFO retention dropped events between pulls); the delivered events resume from the surviving head, so the consumer is told it fell behind. truncated=true means the routing scan hit MAX_BUCKETS_PER_SUBSCRIPTION and more buckets exist than were scanned this pull. Both are flags on a successful response, never an error.

unknown_subscription_error_example._note: An unknown, expired, or malformed sub_id (unknown handle, or reset by a daemon restart) surfaces as the unknown_subscription IPC code. The MCP facade maps every caller-fixable IPC error to JSON-RPC invalid_params (-32602) so the agent corrects its input and keeps routing through Terminal Commander; data.ipc_code carries the typed daemon code verbatim. The remedy is to re-open the subscription.

invariants: sub_id is required; max and timeout_ms are optional
max is clamped to MAX_PULL_EVENTS (omitted = 50); timeout_ms is clamped to [1, MAX_PULL_TIMEOUT_MS 8000] (omitted = DEFAULT_PULL_TIMEOUT_MS 5000)
events are multiplexed across all in-scope buckets and carry bucket_id, optional job_id, the per-bucket seq, and the full SignalEvent
liveness uses the single authoritative Liveness union (snake_case state tag)

US4/FR-031: the adapter requests the liveness delta -- the full snapshot on the first pull and after any seek, then only changed entries, and the section is omitted when the delta is empty; a transition appears in exactly the next pull
an idle pull is SUCCESS with empty events[] (liveness present only as a non-empty delta), never an error
lagged=true flags eviction under the consumer; truncated=true flags the routing scan hitting MAX_BUCKETS_PER_SUBSCRIPTION; both ride a successful response
an unknown, expired, or malformed sub_id returns the unknown_subscription IPC code (mapped to invalid_params on the wire); the caller re-opens
the pull server-advances the per-bucket offsets so it is non-idempotent; the next pull continues from where this one stopped
daemon unavailable is a typed daemon_unavailable error, not an empty success
